name: Manualtrigger Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Manualtrigger Workflow. This workflow integrates 10 different services: stickyNote, hubspot, lmChatGoogleGemini, informationExtractor, set. It contains 19 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: When clicking ‘Test workflow’
nodes.notes: This manualTrigger node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

options.systemPromptTemplate:
=You are a sales representative drafting an email to close a potential customer on the following product: <product>{{ $('Variables').first().json.product_to_sell }}</product>

Use the provided profile to draft the a suitable email which reflects similar communication style and addresses their values, ultimately convinces the customer to inquire about and/or buy this product. Provide only the subject and body of the message as this text will go into a template. Omit the subject and signature.

nodes.notes: This informationExtractor node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## 2. Research Customer via Emails
Emails can be a great source of research on how a customer or potential customer thinks, behaves and communicates. This template does require some interaction beforehand but this should could be shared amongst colleagues or a CRM.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## 3. Build Persona Outline from Research
Once we gather all the emails, we can use AI to analyse and construct a quick persona on our customer. Personas are useful to understand the customer's position and how favourably they might respond to a product and/or service. The Information Extractor node is used to guide the LLM for attributes we're interested in.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## 4. Generate Sales Pitch based on Persona
Using the persona, we can again ask AI to generate the perfect sales email which takes into consideration the customer's beliefs, values and communication style. In this way, each sales email can be carefully written to improve its appeal to the customer.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
## 5. Create Draft for Human Review
Finally, an email draft is created to store the generated sales pitch for human review. If given, a list of customers to target, a SDR can ensure customised outreach in minutes rather than hours or days.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Analyse and Build Persona

options.systemPromptTemplate: =Your task is to build a persona of a customer or potential customer so that we may better serve them for our business. Analyse the recent correspondence of the user, {{ $('Variables').item.json.email }}, and extract the required attributes.

attributes.name: decision_making_style
attributes.description:
=Analytical vs. Intuitive: Do they rely on data or gut feelings?

Risk Appetite: Conservative, calculated risk-taker, or bold?

Speed of Decision-Making: Quick and assertive or deliberate and methodical?

attributes.name: communication_preferences
attributes.description:
=Preferred Medium: Email, phone calls, in-person meetings, messaging apps?

Detail Orientation: High-level summaries or deep-dive explanations?

Tone & Formality: Casual vs. professional, direct vs. diplomatic?

attributes.name: pain_points_challenges
attributes.description:
=Current Business Challenges: What problems are they actively trying to solve?

Industry Pressures: Competitive landscape, economic concerns, regulatory issues?

Operational Bottlenecks: Efficiency, team structure, technology gaps?

attributes.name: professional_goals_motivations
attributes.description:
=Personal Career Goals: Promotion, recognition, financial growth, legacy-building?

Business Priorities: Revenue growth, innovation, market expansion, cost reduction?

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs): How do they measure success?

attributes.name: work_style_preferences
attributes.description:
=Collaboration vs. Independence: Do they prefer teamwork or autonomy?

Level of Involvement: Hands-on or delegate-and-review?

Response Time Expectation: Do they expect immediate follow-ups or are they flexible?

attributes.name: personality_behavioral_traits
attributes.description:
=Big Five Traits: Are they open to new ideas, structured, agreeable, extroverted?

Conflict Resolution Style: Do they avoid, confront, or negotiate?

Trust-Building Factors: Do they value reliability, transparency, exclusivity?

attributes.name: buying_investment_behavior
attributes.description:
=Budget Sensitivity: Price-conscious or value-focused?

Brand Loyalty vs. Openness: Do they stick with familiar providers or explore new options?

Decision Influencers: Do they rely on peers, market research, gut instinct?

attributes.name: preferred_business_culture_ethics
attributes.description:
=Formality vs. Informality: Corporate structure vs. entrepreneurial mindset?

Core Values: Integrity, innovation, customer-first, sustainability?

Cultural Sensitivity: Are there cultural nuances to be aware of in their decision-making?

attributes.name: industry_competitive_awareness
attributes.description:
=Market Trends Interest: Do they actively track industry shifts?

Competitor Awareness: Are they reactive to competitors, or focused on internal growth?

Tech Adoption: Do they embrace innovation, or are they slow adopters?

nodes.notes: This informationExtractor node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Try it out
### This n8n template uses existing emails from customers as context to customise and "finetune" outreach emails to them using AI.

By now, it should be common knowledge that we can leverage AI to generate unique emails but in a way, they can remain generic as the AI lacks the customer context to be truly personalised. One way to solve this is 

### How it works
* Customers to target are pulled from Hubspot and each customer is then run in a loop. We're using a loop as the retrieved emails for each customer become separate items and a loop helps with item reference.
* We connect to our Gmail account to pull all emails recieved from the customer.
* The contents of the email will be suitable to build a short persona of the customer. We use the Information Extractor to get our AI model to pull out the key attributes of this persona such as decision making style and communication preferences.
* With this persona, we can now pass this to our AI model to generate a personalised outreach email specifically for our customer.
* Finally, a draft email is created for human review before sending. If you would rather send the email straight away, this is also possible.

### How to use
* Define the topic of the outreach email in the "variables" node. This directs the AI on what outreach email to generate.
* Ensure the emails are pulled from the right account. If emails may contain sensitive data, adjust the filters and text parsing to ensure these are not leaked to the AI (which might then leak into the generated email).

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
## 1. Get Targeted Existing Customers
As with all campaigns, it's good to have a targeted subset of customers to aim for to assess the response. Here, we can pull them out of a CRM like Hubspot if granular filtering is required for example but even a simple csv of contacts would also work.

parameters.content:
## 2. Research Customer via Emails
Emails can be a great source of research on how a customer or potential customer thinks, behaves and communicates. This template does require some interaction beforehand but this should could be shared amongst colleagues or a CRM.

## 3. Build Persona Outline from Research
Once we gather all the emails, we can use AI to analyse and construct a quick persona on our customer. Personas are useful to understand the customer's position and how favourably they might respond to a product and/or service. The Information Extractor node is used to guide the LLM for attributes we're interested in.

## 4. Generate Sales Pitch based on Persona
Using the persona, we can again ask AI to generate the perfect sales email which takes into consideration the customer's beliefs, values and communication style. In this way, each sales email can be carefully written to improve its appeal to the customer.

## 5. Create Draft for Human Review
Finally, an email draft is created to store the generated sales pitch for human review. If given, a list of customers to target, a SDR can ensure customised outreach in minutes rather than hours or days.

## Try it out
### This n8n template uses existing emails from customers as context to customise and "finetune" outreach emails to them using AI.

By now, it should be common knowledge that we can leverage AI to generate unique emails but in a way, they can remain generic as the AI lacks the customer context to be truly personalised. One way to solve this is 

### How it works
* Customers to target are pulled from Hubspot and each customer is then run in a loop. We're using a loop as the retrieved emails for each customer become separate items and a loop helps with item reference.
* We connect to our Gmail account to pull all emails recieved from the customer.
* The contents of the email will be suitable to build a short persona of the customer. We use the Information Extractor to get our AI model to pull out the key attributes of this persona such as decision making style and communication preferences.
* With this persona, we can now pass this to our AI model to generate a personalised outreach email specifically for our customer.
* Finally, a draft email is created for human review before sending. If you would rather send the email straight away, this is also possible.

### How to use
* Define the topic of the outreach email in the "variables" node. This directs the AI on what outreach email to generate.
* Ensure the emails are pulled from the right account. If emails may contain sensitive data, adjust the filters and text parsing to ensure these are not leaked to the AI (which might then leak into the generated email).

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Palm  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Draft Email For Review" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate Sales Email" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Analyse and Build Persona" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get All Customer's Correspondence" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Contacts" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "For Each Contact" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Contact Ref" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
